evidence.consoleErrors: Only WebGL getSupportedExtensions error from THREE.js (expected, ignored per guidance)

sourceReview.toggleUI: src/pages/AgentDetail.tsx contains a Switch component with aria-label 'Toggle private cognition' bound to agent.private_cognition_enabled
sourceReview.supabaseMutation: togglePrivateCognition useMutation calls supabase.from('agents').update({ private_cognition_enabled: enabled }).eq('id', id) — directly persists to agents table
sourceReview.queryInvalidation: On success, invalidates ['agent-detail', id] query to refresh UI with new value
sourceReview.typeDefinition: src/integrations/supabase/types.ts confirms private_cognition_enabled: boolean in agents table Row, Insert, and Update types

VAL-VENICE-001.notes: Toggle UI renders on agent detail page (verified via agent-browser screenshot showing 'PRIVATE COGNITION Disabled' with Switch control). Source code confirms the toggle fires a Supabase update mutation on the agents table's private_cognition_enabled column. When enabled, a 'Private AI' badge and Venice Model selector also appear. Persistence is verified through source code review: the useMutation calls supabase.from('agents').update({ private_cognition_enabled }) directly.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 1 passed. VAL-VENICE-001 confirmed via agent-browser screenshot showing the private cognition toggle on the agent detail page, plus source code review verifying the Supabase update mutation.
